report_name: Ransomware attacks up 20% year-over-year in H1 2026, says report

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: Identified as the most active ransomware group in Q2 2026 with 299 attacks. The group is known for its sophisticated encryptors, double-extortion tactics, and a strategic shift towards targeting large enterprises.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: Identified as the second most active ransomware group in Q2 2026, responsible for 284 attacks.

threat_actors.name: DragonForce
threat_actors.description: Identified as the third most active ransomware group in Q2 2026, responsible for 147 attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used as a common initial access vector by ransomware groups.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Exploitation of public-facing applications is a common initial access vector for ransomware groups.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Use of stolen credentials for remote access services like RDP and VPNs is a common initial access vector for ransomware groups.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware payloads are deployed to encrypt data after lateral movement as part of double-extortion tactics.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups disable security tools like Microsoft Defender by modifying registry policies to evade detection.

malware.name: Qilin
malware.description: A sophisticated ransomware payload used by the Qilin group in double-extortion attacks.

tools.name: adfind.exe
tools.description: A legitimate Active Directory query tool often abused by ransomware actors for network reconnaissance.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: A legitimate remote administration tool commonly used by ransomware actors for lateral movement and ransomware deployment.

identities.name: Large Enterprises
identities.description: Large enterprises with over $1 billion in revenue, which saw a 74% surge in ransomware attacks, indicating a strategic shift in targeting by sophisticated groups.

identities.name: Small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs)
identities.description: Remain the primary targets for ransomware attacks, although a shift towards larger enterprises is noted.

infrastructure.name: Ransomware Leak Sites
infrastructure.description: Leak sites used by ransomware groups for double-extortion tactics, where exfiltrated data is published.

indicators.name: Disable Microsoft Defender via Registry
indicators.pattern:
title: Disable Microsoft Defender via Registry
id: 2b8c1f9d-3a4e-4b1f-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects command-line activity used to disable Microsoft Defender by modifying its registry policies. This is a common TTP for ransomware groups.
references:
  - https://www.sdcexec.com/safety-security/risk-compliance/news/22970671/nordstellar-ransomware-attacks-up-20-yearoveryear
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\reg.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'add'
      - 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects attempts to disable Microsoft Defender using reg.exe, a technique noted in the report for ransomware groups.

indicators.name: AdFind Execution for Reconnaissance
indicators.pattern:
title: AdFind Execution for Reconnaissance
id: 9d0e1f2a-3b4c-4d5e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of AdFind, a legitimate Active Directory query tool often abused by ransomware actors for reconnaissance.
references:
  - https://www.sdcexec.com/safety-security/risk-compliance/news/22970671/nordstellar-ransomware-attacks-up-20-yearoveryear
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1082
  - attack.discovery
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    OriginalFileName: 'AdFind.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of adfind.exe, a tool noted as being abused by ransomware actors for reconnaissance.

indicators.name: PsExec Service Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: PsExec Service Creation
id: 5a6b7c8d-9e0f-4a1b-8c2d-3e4f5a6b7c8d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of the PsExec service, which is commonly used for lateral movement and ransomware deployment.
references:
  - https://www.sdcexec.com/safety-security/risk-compliance/news/22970671/nordstellar-ransomware-attacks-up-20-yearoveryear
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1570
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: medium
logsource:
  category: service_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ServiceName|startswith: 'PSEXESVC'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the PsExec service, a common tool used for lateral movement by ransomware groups.
